pi_items.source: Task description directly specifies this path.
Task description directly specifies this path.
Code execution (extract_bfi25.py on bfi.csv) writes exactly 27 columns (A1-O5, gender, age) to BFI25.csv.
Since the original dataset has 27 columns, extracting 5 Principal Component scores will add exactly 5 columns, resulting in 32 columns.

privileged_info_summary: The data is derived from the real 'bfi' dataset in the psych R package, so data_is_synthetic is false. The generated BFI25.csv file contains exactly 27 columns, contradicting the metadata claim of 28. Consequently, saving the 5 principal component scores to the dataset yields 32 columns, contradicting the expected 33. Independent R analysis using psych::principal(..., rotate='varimax') confirms the absolute Pearson correlation between the extracted Neuroticism component and Age is approximately 0.108.
